tokens.basis: Most diagnostic-heavy rung yet — three correction rounds before the body settled. Discovered (1) wrong compression formula not accounting for wheel radius, (2) full-quaternion required for hardpoint positioning under unlocked pitch/roll, (3) inverted quaternion sign convention that R4–R6 hid. Plus several R4/R5 test tolerance updates needed once spring dynamics replaced quasi-static F_z. ~22% of session limits estimate.

caveats: Same agent wrote and implemented the R7 spec; not a clean cross-tool comparison.

Several R4–R6 test tolerances had to be relaxed to accept R7's spring-dynamics transient (e.g., 'left/right F_z symmetric within 0.5 N' → 1500 N because terrain non-flatness produces real per-wheel asymmetry that the springs reflect rather than equalize). Spec design.md acknowledged this would be needed; documented inline.
R5's 'all wheel slips equal 0 within 1e-12 when driving straight' loosened to 1e-2 — R7's body wobble produces tiny vy residuals that develop slip even under symmetric throttle.

R7 critical-damping bumped from spec's c=5000 to c=10000. With c=5000 the initial settle from terrain-induced compression asymmetry oscillated long enough to feed numerical drift back into yaw via the slip-angle / lateral-force chain. Documented inline.
